name: EBV_EBER1_EBER2_ncRNA
description: EBV-encoded small RNAs EBER1 (167 nt) and EBER2 (172 nt): RNA polymerase III transcripts expressed at ~10^6 copies per latent cell; the most abundant viral RNAs in EBV latency and the canonical EBV-in-situ hybridization target. Form stable secondary structures resembling adenovirus VA RNAs; bind La autoantigen and L22 ribosomal protein. Both EBERs are essential for B-cell transformation efficiency. Sequence is highly conserved across EBV strains — EBER RT-qPCR is the workhorse for EBV viral load.
expected_copy_count: 1 per genome; ~10^6 mature transcript copies per latent cell
